entry.title: Alert for an critical authorization assignment to the user
entry.content:
Our requirement is to perform a realtime monitoring for the critical profiles assignment for the dialog and non dialog users.To generate an alert when critical profiles are assigned.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SAP for Me Portal Latency Issues Again
entry.content:
Hello Julie,

I would like to follow up with you on this topic. Please could you contact me with more details via email: **REMOVED BY MODERATION** 

Thanks and best regards,

Ozzy

entry.title: Re: Can we create complex transactional FIORI apps using...
entry.content: If you have a complicated validation logic and you want to encapsulate this logic in a ABAP model class, this class is not persistent over serveral roundtrips due to the REST API conecpt. Thus, the validation has to be made on client side, which is very bad, since all the information for validation is on server side. E.g. the concept of restful applications in the context of complicted business transactions with user interaction is a stupid idea.

entry.title: Re: Mark Date field as Nullable in .cds file for CAPM pro...
entry.content: Well, "OnStockDate":"" is as wrong as "OnStockDate":"Z" would be. The UI must send "OnStockDate":null if the date should be null.

entry.title: Re: SAP CRYSTAL REPORT 2020 support ODBC DRIVER 18 FOR SQ...
entry.content:
I believe this post not Answered yet,

On further investigation, we installed the latest crystal report version 2020 and tried “Set Location” and “Verify Database” using the ODBC datasource “ODBC Driver 18 for SQL Server” unable to perform both above activities, even with a new SP created and linked.

However, these activities were working when we use the Datasource configured with “ODBC Driver 17 for SQL Server” .

Hence, It would be helpful, if you could get some technical assistance on launching the Crystal report that uses the stored procedure via the “ODBC Driver 18 for SQL Server”, would help our investigation.

Do SAP Crystal Report have support for SQLProcedures function when Datasource is used with “ODBC Driver 18 for SQL Server”?

- Suresh Balasubramanian

entry.title: Re: Alert for an critical authorization assignment to the...
entry.content:
Through System Monitoring there is a metric called Number of users with critical profiles  under SAP ABAP 7.10 and higher template

Metric description: This is the number of users with critical profiles. By default, all users with profile SAP_ALL are listed here. 

The output of corresponding metric will return text like:

Reference:0ALERT;VIRTUAL; # Items (NonCompliant/Compliant/NotFound/NotValuated/All): XXX/   0/   0/   0/ XXX
To run Configuration Validation use the URL please: <URL>

This metric is not checking particular user, it will review all system users with profile SAP_ALL assigned.

Any other metric behavior will require a custom development, review:

2484872 - Requested metric not included in standard templates

Development of Custom RFC data collector guide

entry.title: Re: April (Citizen) Developer Challenge – SAP Build Apps: Task 1 - S/4HANA OData
entry.content:
I'd just have one question : I wasn't able to fulfill this task until I set the TrustAll property in the Destination Configuration to True. I previously had the "Use default JDK truststore" checkbox set and I wasn't able to fetch the correct data. 

Any ideas on what could I do to be able to complete the exercise without having TrustAll in my destination config?

entry.title: Re: Mark Date field as Nullable in .cds file for CAPM pro...
entry.content: Dear @RalfHandl, Since on UI since I am not entering any value in the date input field, so it is expected that it will take the blank value, and in the payload I can see the blank value. However framework throws error with blank value. How and Why will the UI pass null ?

entry.title: Re: Authenticating Users in The Power Platform (Entra ID)...
entry.content:
This will not work - since you do not only require the certificate but also the corresponding private key (which should never be disclosed).

You seem to be seeking for a Principal Propagation mechanism (in a system-to-system communication).
Maybe OAuth 2.0 (with Authorization Code Grant and PKCE) would be more appropriate.

entry.title: Re: Open SAP GUI in separate window with AIE
entry.content: @JaySchwendemann Probably with the scripting ocx: sapfewse.ocx. But this is only a conjecture.

entry.title: Re: Mark Date field as Nullable in .cds file for CAPM pro...
entry.content: How to make the UI pass null depends on the UI techonology you are using. Please add this information to your question.

entry.title: Re: Mark Date field as Nullable in .cds file for CAPM pro...
entry.content: No, what I mean is, why is it expecting me pass a null value? Can't I simply keep the Date field as blank ?

entry.title: S/4HANA Public Cloud: Read and access restrictions to specific G/L accounts
entry.content:
Hello!

We are wondering how we could give access and read restrictions to heterogeneous set of G/L accounts and hide rest of the COA like Authorization group function in ECC. Though the similar field seems to be available in S4HC, we cannot find possibility to restrict the role regarding it.

We have tried to make these restrictions by Functional area restriction, but it has zero effect to account visibility. Also we tried G/L account hierarchy restriction (HRYTYPE_HRYID_GL), which also did not help.

Is there any other possibilities how we could proceed with this issue?

BR,

Anssi Murtonen

entry.title: Re: Mark Date field as Nullable in .cds file for CAPM pro...
entry.content: What I believe is in Odata we have an option to add a nullable property to our Date fields, if we want to keep it blank, however I see that option is not available here. Is it possible to mark Date field as Nullable so that it accepts blank values as well ?

entry.title: Re: Can we create complex transactional FIORI apps using...
entry.content: so what should be the approach to develop fiori apps for such scenarios? should we remove client side validations, take all the input from the user and send it to backend through multiple odata services. And process the input data in backend and display the result back to client ?

entry.title: Re: MDG-F Hierarchy Assignment with Derivation
entry.content:
Hello,

We have same issue, can anybody help us about hierarchy derivation?

entry.title: Re: Practical introduction to SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine
entry.content:
Thank you for this helpful article. What is the function to_real_value doing with the float array? I.E. how is this vector stored on DB side?  -

In my understanding the interesting point is to find a suitable vector space model for real world data, e.g. tokens from a large corpus, as e.g. word2vec. Storing a vector in a DB is maybe useful but not the main problem.

entry.title: Re: EC Business rule for custom field picklist
entry.content:
Hi Elizabeta,

If you would like to filter on countries then you can link your picklist to a parent picklist. For that you would need to have a custom country field in Comp as well. You can find here more infos:

2861689 - How to create MDF Picklist and set a parent-child relationship between MDF picklists? - SAP for Me

2476080 - Employee Central: Child Picklist not filtered by Country (Parent) - SAP for Me

Furthermore, you can create a custom mdf instead of a picklist and work with associations. I think this is actually the better option, since you can reference your mdf object to a field in Job Info (so country or legal entity does not need to be in Comp). The best option would be to associate your mdf object to Legal entity and enter the following field criteria in the details of your custom field (see screenshot).

Best,

Susan

entry.title: Re: RISE WITH SAP S/4 HANA PUBLIC CLOUD Manage and Classification of Commodity code for a Product.
entry.content:
Hi @former_member818101 , @Babu_Lal_Limba , 

Do you know if exist the possibility to upload the list of the commodity codes for intrastat process (S/4hana public cloud)? Our customer has more than 30K products and need a mass load to register all the stadistical codes. 

Can you help me? 

Or do you know if exist an API to create a custom App (UI5) to create all the commodity codes with a template? I have searched in SAP API Business Hub, and I didn't found. 

Thanks a lot! Regards

#intrastat #s4hanapubliccloud #commoditycode

entry.title: Disconnecting archiving with sendout of documents from SAP
entry.content:
Hello,

we are currently facing a serious problem with our archiving solution, which is also causing failures to send out any emails with invoices from SAP. This is becoming a critical issue for us and we are looking for an alternate temporary solution to the problem.

Is there a possibility to disconnect/unlink the archiving with sendout of docs from SAP? We would like to be able to send out invoices from SAP without archiving them (until the archiving system is available).

Thank you in advance for your advice.

entry.title: Re: March Developer Challenge - CloudEvents: Week 4
entry.content:
Thank you @ajmaradiaga for quick answer and starting services and thank you for great challenge.
Everything worked fine.

SAP GENAI deployment URL is not able to respond the query back. I followed the same tutorial:Prompt LLMs in the generative AI hub in SAP AI Core & Launchpad | SAP Tutorials

any suggestion please?

Thanks,

Padmindra

#genai #LLM #SAPGENAI

entry.title: Connection issue between SAP MII and SAP ECC
entry.content:
Calling FM through SAP MII BLS transaction and getting desired output when executed BLS transaction in MII workbench.

If i called BLS transaction through URL getting "Name or Password incorrect" error.

Checked credentials and connections setting looks good which is used in SAP JCO call.

entry.title: Re: Activity Types and Service Materials for Project Based Services and Project Billing
entry.content:
Hello,

We have the following situation: our customer has intercompany services - assigned in PC as they are not ressource hours based. We are still in the starter system with SAP standard masterdata. 

What I did: I copied activity type U006 to U006Z. I created a material U006Z (copied from U006). Planned the activity cost rates, and the external prices (PSP0). I set up the connection of activity type to material (103637 Maintain Billing Profile). Everything works (I can post the activity, I can see it in both company codes, I see the costs and the planned revenue on the project and I can bill the customer).

What does not work is the Intercompany Billing. For the Intercompany Billing it seems that something is missing - it does not find the material for the pricing. I tried a lot of variances already, but instead of taking the price from my newly created U006Z material, it always pulls T999 miscalenous services and gives a pricing error. But: the miscalenous services have always the amount of the PC I posted.

Any idea what could be missing?

Best regards
Anja

entry.title: Re: March Developer Challenge - CloudEvents: Week 4

I'm a little late but better late than never.

entry.title: Re: March Developer Challenge - CloudEvents: Week 4
entry.content: Glad to know that you enjoyed the challenge.

entry.title: KANBAN issue straight to cost center after status FULL
entry.content:
Dear Gurus,

I am looking for an advice what is required in order to achieve when KANBAN card is set to FULL it will automatically issue material to COST CENTER.

The current scenario:

Supply area X is assign to storage location AAA which is bulk storage location via PK05

Control cycle created with replenishment strategy Y which is tied with warehouse storage location BBB.

Two KANBAN status maintained as a full or empty.

Storage type is tied with storage location AAA with stock transfer 311

For replenishment strategy we use as below:

When the card is set to empty it will issue the stock from bulk location AAA to cost center, then TR/TO is created with replenishment stock to replaced within the same location, next time the card is set to empty it will again issue the stock on bulk location and stock transfer another quantity.

The goal is to change it, when status is set to full it would automatically issue stock to cost center without any stock transfer. I have tried to change under replenishment strategies "Procurement for cost center" instead of "Post withdrawal" but without a success.

entry.title: Disconnecting archiving with sendout of documents from SAP
entry.content:
Hello,

we are currently facing a serious problem with our archiving solution, which is also causing failures to send out any emails with invoices from SAP. This is becoming a critical issue for us and we are looking for an alternate temporary solution to the problem.

Is there a possibility to disconnect/unlink the archiving with sendout of docs from SAP? We would like to be able to send out invoices from SAP without archiving them (until the archiving system is available).

Thank you in advance for your advice.

entry.title: Re: The requests received by the Report Processing Server have the format of an old version
entry.content:
This post shows CR4R is becoming EOL, So not sure if making the report in a lower version then updating CR and then re saving would be an issue or not?

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/moving-from-crystal-reports-for-enterprise-to-crystal-reports/ba-p/13579905

entry.title: Re: Practical introduction to SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine
entry.content:
Thanks. I don't think SAP released info on the internal representation of these vectors. My best guess at the moment is they store it as some sort of special blob because if you just SELECT the table you get a binary representation of them (but you can do to_nvarchar() on them and get back the float values as text).

It is true that for a RAG scenario HANA Cloud DB is only giving you the ability to efficiently store, retrieve and query vector for similarity. The conversion of tokens/document chunks into embeddings needs to be done elsewhere.

entry.title: Re: Can you remember the first time you heard of SAP
entry.content:
Hello Katherine!

  We have had offline chats about blog content moderation, and I wanted to share here about the 3 types of reviewers an author might encounter: moderators, owners, and curators. All content is subject to moderation; some topic spaces may have additional rules deemed by owners and/or curators. Did I get that right?

entry.title: Talent Intelligence Hub - Myths and Misconceptions - Part 2 (Migration)
entry.content:
Talent Intelligence Hub (TIH) enables customers to capture the knowledge, abilities, and experience of their workforce and leverage that information to fuel all their talent management processes.

While working with customers and partners, I came across numerous questions, myths, and misconceptions. This blog is the second of a three-part series that aims to highlight the most common misunderstandings that I encountered and provide the necessary insights to debunk those myths.

Part 2 focusses on the myths about the migration to Talent Intelligence Hub. There appears to be some uncertainty about what exactly happens when you hit the button to start the migration.

If you missed the first part of this series, called Talent Intelligence Hub - Myths and Misconceptions - Part 1 (Prerequisites), you can find it here.

 

Myth #1 – Job Profile Builder (JPB) goes away when you turn on TIH

This is probably the most common myth I have heard of so far. I would like to address this misconception thoroughly in order to debunk it once and for all. If you are using JPB and you migrate to Talent Intelligence Hub, JPB remains your job architecture framework and will not be eliminated.

I believe there may be some confusion because there are currently three different situations a customer can be in regarding their competency and job architecture framework, as illustrated below.

 

Let’s take a quick look at these three scenarios:

1. Job Description Manager (JDM)

The oldest framework of all currently existing frameworks is used to manage both the job architecture (legacy job families and job roles) and legacy competency libraries.

2. Center of Capabilities (CoC) and Job Profile Builder (JPB)

If you migrated to JPB after the introduction of CoC or deliberately turned on CoC, you are managing your job architecture and legacy skills through JPB and your competencies through Center of Capabilities.

3. Talent Intelligence Hub (TIH) and Job Profile Builder (JPB)

This is the ideal state for a customer’s system, where competencies, skills and other attribute types are managed through TIH while the job architecture remains in JPB.

 

In principle, there is a fourth possibility, which is managing your job architecture on JDM and migrating to TIH for managing competencies and skills. While this is technically possible, we highly recommend that all customers move away from the old frameworks and manage their job architecture in JPB and their competencies and skills in TIH.

 

Myth #2 - You can transfer your legacy skills to TIH

As explained above, customers who are on Job Profile Builder and Center of Capabilities have access to the legacy skills feature. If you are actively using this feature, meaning you have skill data in JPB, you can’t migrate legacy skills to TIH, regardless of whether you are working with this data or not.

Currently, we cannot migrate legacy skills from JPB to TIH. Therefore, any customers using legacy skills need to take the following steps:

  • Remove all connections between the legacy skills and job roles, job profiles, employees etc.
  • Once all connections have been removed, delete the legacy skills from JPB

If you wish to continue working with these skills and have a history of skill ratings, you have two options. You can either wait for the migration to become possible or create backups of the skill data and skill ratings. After the successful migration, you can import these backups to TIH.

 

Myth #3 - TIH is only available on certain data centers

This misconception is likely caused by the numerous recent AI innovations, some of which are currently restricted to certain data centers, such as Joule.

Fortunately, I have good news for you! Talent Intelligence Hub is accessible in all data centers and can be activated through the Upgrade Center.

 

Myth #4 - Enabling TIH only requires a single click of a button

The honest response is: It depends! 😉

In principle, it is easy to migrate to Talent Intelligence Hub. As mentioned above, all you need to do is navigate to the Upgrade Center, select Talent Intelligence Hub and click the button that will trigger the migration.

However, it is highly likely that there is some data cleanup you’ll need to perform first or a setting that needs to be enabled before you can switch on TIH. This is why the Check Tool is your best friend!

If you’ve never worked with the Check Tool before, simply think of it as a built-in tool in SuccessFactors for performing system health checks that can uncover issues related to data and configuration and provide guidance on how to fix them. A detailed guide can be found here.

Before you start the migration, I highly recommend performing all checks first and resolving any issues that the Check Tool finds. These checks are:

1. In the “System Health” tab, run the checks for Center of Capabilities, Job Description Manager and Job Profile Builder

2. In the “Migration” tab, run the checks for Center of Capabilities, Job Description Manager and Talent Intelligence Hub

Most check results can be resolved by customers without any external help. In a few cases, however, you might need to reach out to your implementation partner or SAP.

Only once all the Check Tool results are positive can you successfully migrate to TIH.

 

My next blog will be the last part of this three-part series and cover myths and misconceptions related to Talent Intelligence hub features and functionality.

Is there anything else you’re not sure about regarding the migration to TIH? Please share your questions via the comments section.

entry.title: Re: The SAP Cloud Platform SDK for Android is GA!
entry.content: Exciting news! The SAP Cloud Platform SDK for Android has officially reached General Availability (GA)! This powerful tool empowers developers to build robust Android applications seamlessly integrated with SAP Cloud Platform services. With features like offline data synchronization, push notifications, and secure authentication, developers can create scalable and enterprise-grade apps with ease. While this SDK caters to Android developers, SAP also offers solutions for iOS Application Developers , ensuring a cohesive experience across platforms. Embrace the future of mobile app development with SAP Cloud Platform SDK and unlock endless possibilities for innovation and efficiency in your enterprise applications.

entry.title: How to maintain number range by selecting object in S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
SNRO option is not available in the Public Cloud.  How can we maintain number range by selecting Object Name: MATERIALNR?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to maintain number range by selecting object in S...
entry.content: In S/4HANA Cloud, T-CODE SNRO is unavailable, please use SSCUI 102282 "Define Number Ranges for Each Material Type" to maintain material number range.

entry.title: Navigating the Data-Driven World of Procurement Analytics: The Roadmap to Achieving Best-In-Class St
entry.content:
In the rapidly progressive digital era, procurement analytics mastery has emerged as a crucial strategic asset, providing crucial insights for enhancing business efficiency and fortifying performance. But how does one truly excel in this integral domain? This guide outlines a comprehensive pathway to achieving elite status in procurement analytics.

Data Quality and Governance: The Cornerstone of Analytical Mastery

The first step to mastering procurement analytics involves effective data management. Access to accurate, complete, consistent, and timely data is vital for insightful analysis. A study by The Hackett Group cites that 70% of businesses find their data quality impeding their ability to capitalise on digital platforms. The implementation of concrete policies, standards, and protocols for data collection, validation, integration, storage, and protection significantly enhances your analytical prowess.

Blend and Integrate Data Sources

Judicious procurement decision-making demands access and fusion of data from a plethora of internal and external sources. These may encompass comprehensive data comprising procurement, finance, sales, inventory, customer, supplier, ERP, market, and industry specifics. Research in the Journal of Theoretical and Applied Electronic Commerce has revealed that productive data unification results in annual savings of 3% for businesses. Employing tools such as data warehouses, data lakes, APIs, and ETL processes can optimise this integration process.

Harness the Potential of Data Analysis and Visualisation

Extracting insightful data, the crux of procurement analytics, using analytical techniques like descriptive, diagnostic, predictive, prescriptive analytics and graphical representations such as dashboards, reports, charts and maps, aid in drawing these insights. Gartner reveals that businesses utilising visual data discovery are apt to find timely information 28% more than others.

Nurturing a Data-Driven Culture along with Capability Enhancement

A successful data analytics strategy encompasses more than just sophisticated tools; it requires a shift to a data-driven corporate culture. This shift has been ratified by a NewVantage Partners survey, with 92.2% of businesses acknowledging people and processes as the major roadblocks to adopting Big Data and AI in daily operations. Emphasise on honing the skills of procurement staff and partners while encouraging data usage and analytics in day-to-day tasks and strategic planning can promote a culture emphasising curiosity, collaboration, learning, and innovation.

With the roadmap clearly laid out above, remember that the journey to mastering procurement analytics is dependent on quality data, wholesome data integration, solid data analysis and visuals, and an unwaveringly data-centric organisational culture. Keeping these aspects in focus will enable your procurement analysis efforts to push your company to new heights amidst stringent market competition.

In conclusion, resource investment in honing your procurement analytics capabilities will not only help you keep pace with rapid advancements, but also firmly position you at the vanguard of best-in-class performers. Don't miss the opportunity to bolster your procurement strategy with data analytics today and embrace the challenges of the future with confidence.

Feel free to read my previous blog on Boosting Business Success with Procurement Analytics: A Journey Paved with Numbers

SAP Ariba Spend Analysis

entry.title: Re: Can you remember the first time you heard of SAP
entry.content:
Hello Jim,

yes, but the owners and curators relate to SAP Community Groups only, not the general SAP Community blog boards - so the onus is on those roles in those "spaces" to review and approve content for their group. Helps to get the right content in the right space!

entry.title: Re: Error message
entry.content:
Hi Tonio,

I would recommend to direct your inquiry to our SAP IT to accurately identify the problem and provide better recommendation for the solution. As I understand if you are using an SAP provided laptop then SAP Logon App is available in Software Center App. But if it is different then your local IT should be able to help.

I hope this helps!
Paulo M.

Hi Dan,

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP TRIAL ABAP ENVIRONMENT ISSUES
entry.content: Could you check whether note https://me.sap.com/notes/3254529/E is helpful for you?

entry.title: AI Core - problem with copying object from AWS to /mnt/models
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I am new to AI Core product, and I am trying to make some scenarios, but I faced problem that is stopping me for a while now.

 

Background:

I have been doing tutorial about SAP BTP AI Core: https://developers.sap.com/group.ai-core-get-started-basics.html

Everything was working fine till the tutorial “Make Predictions for House Prices with SAP AI Core”: https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/ai-core-deploy.html

There is step about to loading trained model to make predictions (I will refer to this later):
model = pickle.load(open ('/mnt/models/model.pkl','rb'))

Then there is the rest of the code for creating endpoints and using this model for prediction.

Further configuration I did correct (Docker, yaml etc.), according to tutorial.

The service started but was not working (error 500 when calling endpoint).

 

My tests and analysis:

I couldn’t find any potential problem for that, I rechecked everything and the web service from tutorial was still not working.

I decided to create my own model and configuration for a very simple example of model which is instance of class making addition (very simple one, just for tests).

But this time I created some more endpoints for this web service to check if problem is only with the one using (and loading) model:

Again, everything was working except the part of using the model, so:

  • The AWS S3 Bucket has this model (I check it manually using console) and path is correct.
  • Python code is fine because I successfully tested it locally.
  • Git with YAML file is correct because the service is running and other endpoints working.
  • All actions in AI Core (deployments, executions etc.) were also successful.

I implemented more functionalities into this webservice, so I was able to confirm that all other endpoints were working correctly and only the one loading model was returning error 500.

One of the functionalities was defined as this:

And the output was:
AI Core Server: __pycache__|main.py|requirements.txt|

So basically, there is no other folder (should be /mnt) or file in this location… Of course, I don’t know how exactly AI Core works internally, so maybe this approach for testing is wrong, but I also don’t know how else I can check this, when I see this log:

“INFO:root:Successfully copied s3://hcp-… to /mnt/models”

The location is 100% correct, I can see (and copy) this model using direct access to my AWS S3 Bucket. I did this and deployed locally, and it is working fine.

My conclusion is that the model is not copied successfully (the path doesn’t even exist), or I am making some error in the implementation of this functionality.

 

Question:s

  1. Is my approach for testing this correct (should this python function return all files and folders for my instance of AI Core in root location /)?
  2. How else I can debug/analyze this problem? Have you ever faced a similar issue?
  3. Is it possible that the free version of AI Core has some limitations like this or a hidden non-deterministic  bug?

I fount very similar problem here:
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/sap-ai-core-error-while-prediction/qaq-p/12769557

This problem has not been resolved on that forum as I see. I followed hints from there but with no results.

 

Best regards

entry.title: Re: DDMRP setup SAP S4HANA GUI
entry.content:
Hi, the DDMRP has below APPs, and all these can work only if you have the FI

 

  • Schedule Product Classification (DD) (F2823)
  • Mass Maintenance of Products (DD) (F2825)
  • Buffer Positioning (F3282)
  • Schedule Lead Time Classification of Products (DD) (F2871)
  • Schedule Buffer Proposal Calculation (F2837)
  • Manage Buffer Levels (F2706)
  • Maintain PIRs (F3445)
  • Schedule pMRP Simulation Creation (F3968)
  • Process pMRP Simulations (F3934)

entry.title: Custom data as table, CDS, Domain, Business object and all that jazz...
entry.content:
The need for custom data is nothing new. But these days we want to do it while keeping our core clean and thus we want to look at Key User and on-stack Developer Extensibility, which give a lot of options. Below I attempt to showcase each option relating to having your own "custom data" in S/4HANA, with some pros and cons and when to use which option. To illustrate the options, I'll be doing a bookshop scenario; my company is a bookshop, I have an S/4HANA system and now I need to extend it.

 

Custom transactional or master data

If you have a need for custom transactional or master data, I would recommend to first look at Custom Business Objects app (App on SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library). Meaning, if in the old ways you would have created z-table(s) and then generated SAP GUI sm30 maintenance dialog (or even your own ABAP report for maintenance), then you want to use this.

This app lets us create our own "object", meaning data that has more than just 1 field. In the example of the bookshop, authors of books could be such a custom object. Custom Business Objects even lets us create hierarchical objects; say for example we even would like to be able to enter one or more family member of that author, we would not want to do it on the same "level" as the author, but we would have author and then 0 or more family members linked to that author. This is done very easily in Custom Business Objects, as all you need to do is add an extra "Node":

And under "Fields" we enter the needed fields for both nodes:

Important before we publish this, if this is data that needs a "maintenance dialog" or a Fiori app which allows us to create, update, and delete entries for this data, then you want to check under Features "User Interface" if you are on S/4HANA Public Cloud. If not, this option will not be available, and you instead manually check "Back End Service" (which is checked automatically after selecting User Interface for Public Cloud).

As seen above, if we need to add some logic before we want to save data (on update and before save), we can also check "Determination and Validation" (only if we need that). 

After publishing, we can write the needed logic (if any at all), we can look at our generated Fiori app, and we can create a tile for it, so we can add it to the Fiori Launchpad of the users needing this app. On Private Cloud or On Premise we have a few extra steps to take; using the generated OData service (its name is listed underneath "Back End Service"), we can generate our own Fiori Elements Application on SAP BTP with Business Application Studio. The con here is of course that we need to do this as an extra step. The pro is that we can fully decide how the UI should look like. Once we are done with the UI, we can deploy the app to our system, generate tile and target mapping for it and assign to users, as per usual practice.

Hopefully you can see here that we get a lot of functionality "out of the box" while using Custom Business Object's simple user interface. We define our fields, have the possibility for hierarchical data and for public cloud we can even generate a whole Fiori app. This option should therefore be considered first, before you go and create your custom z-table(s) in the dictionary. If you do it that way, you are many steps away from a Fiori App and an old-school SM30 maintenance dialog is not clean core.

Here a screenshot of the final app, which I generated via BAS on SAP BTP without needing to change anything on the UI (all done via Fiori Tools wizard):

What if the data needs NO maintenance app?

As I mentioned, Custom Business Object is perfect when we want to replace old z-tables with sm30. But what if we had z-tables that were filled differently? Maybe from an integration or through a BAdI? Well, the answer is "it depends" 😉. When we talk about integration, we can use the generated CDS to create a REST API service that could be used by the integration. If we create z-table(s), we have to do the CDS part manually. If a REST API won't do the trick, consider how you update the data? In the ABAP Cloud programming model, we don't want to MODIFY/UPDATE/INSERT directly into the database, not even z-tables. Instead we use EML or Entity Manipulation Language, for example:

 

 

DATA update_tab TYPE TABLE FOR UPDATE /DMO/R_AgencyTP.
update_tab = VALUE #( ( agencyID = '070001' Name = 'MODIFIED Agency' ) ).
MODIFY ENTITIES OF /DMO/R_AgencyTP " name of behavior definition
  ENTITY /DMO/Agency " name of entity to read
  UPDATE FIELDS ( name )
  WITH update_tab.
COMMIT ENTITIES.

 

 

So once again, having a CDS generated can actually save time here. But there are cases where we want to be in full control of the CDS RAP object, then we could start from z-tables and work our way all the way to CDS and behavior definitions and create everything manually; takes time but it's powerful and flexible.

 

Custom "Name-Value" data

If you need a simple field where you have a couple of static values (which don't change often and don't need any maintenance app) with a descriptive text, then in the old days we would create a domain. Today we can still do it with a domain, or we can use "Code Lists" as they are called in the Key User apps. Code Lists are explained in the next chapter and can be created as "reusable", meaning those code lists are used on custom business object, or directly when adding a custom field to a business context. To showcase the example, I created a domain for the bookshop:

Further below I add a custom field "Book Genre" into Product General Data and book genre uses this domain. So, if the domain has no direct end user exposure, it is very fast and easy to keep using. However, if I would have added the field directly onto Product General Data, then a Code List would have been preferable, because the input will be managed with drop-down that can be typed in, rather than having to open a pop-up for search-help and finding the domain values.

 

Extend Standard Data with Custom Data

Often just creating our custom data is not enough; we want to somehow combine it with SAP Standard. Here we have several options:

Case 1: Single Fields to be saved on same level as standard

If you have single, non-connected fields to add to a SAP Standard object, we want to use Custom Fields (App on SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library) app and just add field-by-field. Coming back to the bookshop example and the author; we would not want to create field "Author First Name", and another field, "Author Last Name" and then add both to the Product Master Data, for example. But, we could add a field called "Number of Pages of Book", numeric, to the product master data:

Case 2: Single Field where only certain values (from a drop-down) should be selected

When we have a field where we want to restrict what the user enters, we can again use Custom Fields app, and choose as field type "Code List". This lets us then maintain the possible values for users to enter directly in the Custom Fields app. In our example it could be the format of the book:

These values can of course be translated.

The 2nd option is to create a z-Domain/z-table with values in the dictionary, then create a CDS view as value help for those values, and then use that CDS view in Custom Fields, when selecting "Code List based on CDS View". I did this example for Book Genres:

This is in fact great as a "not so good" example. So we have a z-table but we won't create a maintenance dialog for sm30, because it is not clean core. Above I've set "@AbapCatalog.dataMaintenance" as #ALLOWED, meaning we can simply maintain the data in se16n, but also meaning we have no automatic transport of data and so on, and no regular user can do it, nor is it easy to control authorization for users who should be able to maintain this. So the book genre would have been better solved as Custom Business Object, but I wanted to show different options and also to highlight why a z-table maybe isn't the best solution. Next, I have to create CDS views that I can then use in Custom Fields and I need to 2, in order to follow the ABAP Cloud programming model:

  1. Basic interface view for reading form the database table
  2. Value help view using the basic interface view

Only now can I got to Custom Fields and add the Book Genre to Product master data:

Wait: what about Custom Reusable Elements - Code List?

If you are familiar with the key user apps you may know of Custom Reusable Elements (App on SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library). We know this app has custom libraries, meaning ABAP code, but also Code Lists. Having talked about using Code Lists in custom fields, when would be use this one then? This is simply put for Custom Business Objects. If your custom business object has a field where you want the restricted values functionality, you would first create it in this app. For example we could have a "popularity" field on the Author custom business object and we would want only few possible values:

When we add a field now in our custom business object (any custom business object), we select as type "Code List" and then pick one.

Case 3: Link Custom Object with Standard

If we have more than just single, disconnected fields, but instead we want to link a Standard object to our own data that has it's own table or even hierarchical tables (as in our author example), then we still use Custom Fields app. In Custom Fields as we add a new field, we can choose "Association to Business Object". If we do, we need to select the business object in the drop down, which includes all our Custom Business Object. For example:

Case 4: Combining data for reports

If our case is not to save master or transactional data, but instead use some custom data for reports, we have several options.

  1. Use Custom CDS Views (Version 2) (App on SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library) to combine existing CDS views together for reporting (if your custom data has no CDS view yet, which it will have if you used Custom Business Object, you can get a developer to create one for you, option 3)
  2. Use Custom Analytical Queries (App on SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library) if you need analytical capabilities with measures, dimensions, calculations, and so on. This app needs an analytical cube or query CDS, which a developer can create for you if not yet existing - option 3
  3. Use on-stack development to create your own custom CDS views (analytical or transactional) and create any report and combination of standard and custom CDS views. This option can be used together with the first 2 above, or can even be used to create a custom reporting Fiori app with BAS on SAP BTP. This is the most powerful of all the options and allows for a lot freedom on creating exactly what you need. This can also be used if the data needs to be exposed as API to integrations or other purposes

 

Summary

I'm sure there are lots more cases for custom data and I doubt I can list them all. But the above show cases how to do it with key user apps and how they can help to do it faster, simpler, and have a nice user interface for the end user with input help. If you have a specific case you wonder about, just drop a comment and ask 😊 Hope this was helpful!

entry.title: Re: Mark Date field as Nullable in .cds file for CAPM pro...
entry.content: Dear @RalfHandl, Is it necessary to pass null for Date field, is it not possible to add annotation in CDS so that it will accept blank/empty values?

entry.title: Re: SIC Code in Plant level
entry.content: Fields MVGR1 up to MVGR5 in MVKE table are freely definable fields - this is not SIC code by default, but something specific to your system. MARC table (plant level) does not contain such fields. You can add new Z-fields via an append on the MARC table if needed.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA 2022 Fully-Activated Appliance: Known Issues
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

To run the JAVA server on SUSE Linux 15 we needed to follow the note 3018138. With the trace flag applied, the ASE DB worked fine.

3018138 - Server fails to boot after signal 11 with Snap::Validate - SAP ASE 16.0 - SAP for Me

Kind regards
Jakob

entry.title: Request failed with status code 403 in sap in Business application studio.
entry.content:
Hello SAP Experts,

I have developed SAP Fiori report apps in BAS getting below error while deploying it on ABAP repository. i have cross-check with ABAP developer as well but not able to identify the root cause. Please look into this issue and provide solution ASAP.

below is the error which display while deploying app.

error builder:custom deploy-to-abap Request failed with status code 403 in sap

Thank you in advance

entry.title: Re: Mark Date field as Nullable in .cds file for CAPM pro...
entry.content: If you define an element in CAP CDS without the "not null" phrase, it is nullable. Which means it will accept JSON null as a value, which is different from an empty JSON string. There is no magic switch to treat empty strings identical to null.

entry.title: Re: Convert Journal Voucher to Journal Entry via DI-API
entry.content:
Hi Pipalia1,

No, you cannot create Journal Entry from Journal Voucher as Journal Voucher is not having any GetbyKey() method.

You should use JournalEntries object in DI API to create the Journal Entry.

Hope it helps!

Kind regards,

ANKIT CHAUHAN

SAP Business One Support

entry.title: Structured Flatfile (txt) in Datasphere
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I assume it is simple but I couldn't really find a solution. I have txt-flatfiles with a fixed field length for each column. NOT comma seperated or any othr seperation. 

I just couldn't find a standard way to integrate these on an automated daily base.

Any experiences here?

Thanks!

Peter

entry.title: Add Custom Action to Object Page Header toolbar using BAS Adaptation.
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

Please guide me to add Custom Action to Object Page Header toolbar using BAS Adaptation. I have seen and followed the article :

https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/sdk/#/topic/7619517a92414e27b71f02094bd08d06

and seen the sample :

https://ui5.sap.com/test-resources/sap/fe/core/fpmExplorer/index.html#/controllerExtensions/controllerExtensionsOverview

but the problem is: In adaptation project we dont have manifest.json but manifest.appdescr_variant , where when I am doing changes it is not working , how can i proceed with it.

Any help is highly appreciated.

Regards

Rakesh

entry.title: Re: Convert Journal Voucher to Journal Entry via DI-API
entry.content: Another one for enhancements sir. Thank you kindly.

thank you for the blog, if we switch from Classic to Advanced, in which part of the new job the valuation area and method are taken into consideration? This is no clear to me, in the job we only have the company code.

Thanks

Martina

entry.title: Are there any recommendations / restriction of number of fields in a CDS view?
entry.content: Will it be ok to create "big" CDS views with thousands of fields from multiple tables / interface CDS views that should be used for ad-hoc reports in SE16x transactions?

entry.title: Re: Request failed with status code 403 in sap in Business application studio.
entry.content: The server is responding with code 403 which means access to the endpoint is forbidden. Check the setup and logs on the server side.

entry.title: Re: Workbench request can not transport to target
entry.content:
Hi,

you have to set the target system at properties also you can check in master request's tasks. It may contain work belonging to another system so you have to change TADIR value.

Regards.

Ali Polat.

entry.title: Back to basics with SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP) - part 10
entry.content: In this episode, we move from exploring associations towards compositions. What's the difference? How are they similar? What do they look like behind the curtain? Everyone welcome, especially beginners, come and join us for friendly chat and curiosity-driven learning-by-doing!

entry.title: Re: April (Citizen) Developer Challenge – SAP Build Apps: Task 1 - S/4HANA OData
entry.content:
Hello Dan,

7e778659fd7f8f8c03bf6cfe8d567b9e2ecb80904d7f259ad8b0b152e8813315

Name of the event is "SAP BUILD CodeJam"

entry.title: Re: Request failed with status code 403 in sap in Busines...
entry.content: Didn't you find any guided answer for "SAP Fiori tools" "Deployment to ABAP System"  or a KBA such as 3424792 - Deployment from BAS to ABAP system fails ?

entry.title: Re: Understanding the Price Category information
entry.content:
Hi @APATHAK 

This information is only valid for 3-SL systems where the catalogs are created by the customer.

In 2-SL Custom Catalog Extensions, as you mentioned, the Standard Catalogs are being used and therefore they follow the pricing category as default.

Hope I could clarify your concerns.

 

Kind regards.

entry.title: Re: Batch creation in error using 262 movement
entry.content:
In standard it is not there, we need to use the userexit / Badi to evaluate thh batch number validation and stock is there for it

BR,

dsk

entry.title: Workflow CC Role email notification issue
entry.content:
Hello, 

I have a situation with workflow and CC roles where, if I assign multiple CC Roles (wf group, Role, email address) and each of these roles has custom made email template group, user that is member of more than one CC Role gets only one email, the one that is first on the list. 

If i remove that user, or add someone else, shortly - if all CC Roles have different users, all email are beeing sent. If user is in 2 or more CC Roles, he doesn't get those 2 or more emails, but only one. 
Is this some kinf of limitation or something else is going on here?
My workflow is not connected to business rule, only to time type (sick leave). The company policy is, sick leave requests don't go to approval process but only notification emails must be sent to employee initiator, team lead, ceo and hr. To get that, 4 different email templates which are created trough document generation management are assigned to proper CC roles in email template group.

entry.title: Re: Disconnecting archiving with sendout of documents fro...
entry.content:
Output condition records could be switched from "Print and Archive" to just "Print" while you are working on your archive problem.  It can also be done at the configuration level but that requires transports that would need to be reversed later.

 

Regards,

Ryan Crosby

entry.title: Re: SAP Deployment over Intune - Auto Update failed
entry.content:
Hey!

How did you know the installation failed, what error message appears? Which software is throwing the error?

The SAP GUI installation check would help you to find errors occurred during the installation. You can follow the step by step: SAP GUI options > System information > Check SAP GUI installation

Or follow the KBA 2881681 - Tutorial "How to run a SAP Frontend Installation Check?" [VIDEO]

 

Hope the logs can clarify better what is the issue you're facing during the update.

 

 

All the best,

Lucas

entry.title: Re: Same Mapping Different WSDL
entry.content: The warning is letting you know that source fields in the mapping no longer exist in the new WSDL. It will handle adjustment if the core structure for mapped fields does not change, but that must not be so in your case. The only option with a big change to the source is to redo the mapping.

entry.title: Re: SAP Data services
entry.content: Thank you for the prompt response sir

entry.title: How to Update First Name - Univeral ID
entry.content:
Hi SAP,

Can someone guide me how to update first name in universal id.

 

Regards,

Mohammed

entry.title: Re: Disconnecting archiving with sendout of documents fro...
entry.content: Hello Ryan,

entry.title: Re: How to Update First Name - Univeral ID
entry.content: I would like to give little background of the issue. I am unable to take up the sap certifications as the first name in universal id is not matching with my government id.

entry.title: Re: Disconnecting archiving with sendout of documents fro...
entry.content: Hello Ryan, thank you very much for your quick response. I have quickly checked out config and I do not see any SSCUI with such option. Did you have any specific SSCUI in mind? Or where could it be switched?

entry.title: Re: Same Mapping Different WSDL
entry.content: I solved it by uploading XSD file. I was replacing XSD via WSDL, which will not work, off course, as the mapping cannot match old xsd configurations with the new WSDL configurations

entry.title: Re: Issue with SAP BTP Destination Rest API Integration [SAP Build Apps]
entry.content: @agpekka/ @joycesoares Joycesoares - Did you get any success in POSTing file though App?

entry.title: Re: Document 100000000 does not exist in company code 401...
entry.content: Document number 100000003 2024 was already assigned

entry.title: Re: Excel uploading error msg
entry.content:
Hello Ioannis,

You're using SAP GUI 8.00 32 or 64bit? And your Office?

If you're using SAP GUI 8.00 64bit we recommend the usage of MS Office in the same architecture.

 

In addition, I would check if the issue occurs with multiple workstations or only with specific ones.

In case it is workstation specific, check what is the difference of these workstations.

 

If nothing of these recommendations helped you to solve this issue, you can create a case on SAP Support reporting your issue. This issue should be handled at the first by BC-FES-GUI or BC-FES-OFFI team to collect the required traces.

 

All the best,

Lucas Machado

entry.title: Re: Mark Date field as Nullable in .cds file for CAPM pro...
entry.content: @RalfHandl, but I also have fields of type String and Integer which are defined with Not Null Phrase, but when empty Json string is accepted here, but same is not true Date field. I am using SAPUI5 as frontend, and I think it is giving empty strings as default for input fields

entry.title: Delta loading in SAP CPI based on the ABAP CDS view
entry.content:
Hello experts!

I have an integration request that needs to send "new" information from our S/4 Hana to an external system, using SAP CPI as middleware.

My first idea was to ask the ABAP team to create an ODATA service pointing to the data I have to get and handle the deltas using a timestamp field. So, every time I query the service, I'll add a condition like $filter=DTField > [LastValueStored in CPI].

Even when this approach will meet our requirements, I've seen that CDS views can include an annotation called "Change Data Capture", that allows you to handle the delta loadings in a more "transparent" way.

I find a lot of articles about this feature, and how to use it, but no one using CPI. I mean, it's clear that there's not a "CDS sender adapter", but I couldn't find how to use an ODATA service, created from a CDS view with this annotation, that can be queried from CPI.

I found this article: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/delta-loading-in-sap-datasphere-based-on-the-abap-cds-view/ba-p/13575700 and in there is mentioned something " ODP framework". Is this something that can be used with CPI?

Any piece of information about this will be very welcomed.

Regards

Adrián

entry.title: Re: Disconnecting archiving with sendout of documents fro...
entry.content:
This is an example from our system if you are using condition based output.  If you have records that are set for print and archive a config change may not work directly and you could still be stuck changing condition records (would have to test it). 

 

Regards,

Ryan Crosby

entry.title: Re: What You Need to Know About Blogging Post-Migration
entry.content:
Hello Team,

I am unable to edit my blog-post. With the new platform all the alignment has gone wrong and I am unable to edit, could you please help me here?

entry.title: Re: Cannot retrieve session with VBA
entry.content:
Hello!

The following KBA can help you to activate Scripting in SAP GUI:

#2296251 - How can SAP GUI Scripting be enabled?

 

All the best,

Lucas

entry.title: Re: Assign a user to the cell in SAP SAC
entry.content: Cell commenting doesnt work for us because of conflicts between different hierarchies and filters on different pages (comments entered on one page don't appear on the other one). Dimension comments work good so far, however we lack the functionality of notifying users for example in the chat or email with the @User assignments, pointing to the specific cell in teh table. In Google docs such things seems super easy and handy. I find it disappointing that SAC doesnt allow such thing (or I didnt find such solution yet).

entry.title: Re: PaPM Cloud: how reproduce on-premise run manager func...
entry.content: Hi Paulo! thank you for response.So the only way to test a specific function, using a specific process ID (with selection and parameters) from modeling view, is to create a Process Template, even if i don't have to deploy it because my porpouse is only to test?

entry.title: Re: Mark Date field as Nullable in .cds file for CAPM pro...
entry.content:
An empty string is a string and it is not null.

An "empty" integer typically is zero which again is different from null.

Unfortunately I don't know how to tell SAPUI5 to treat an empty date field as null.

entry.title: Re: Using OpenAI Models in SAP Build Apps
entry.content:
Hi @patrosrinivasa 

I guess I am a bit too late, but maybe other readers experience the same.

It's importants to use the different output ports of the HTTP request. If the Alert and ongoing process use the same port, it didn't work out for me. So I put both 'Set variables'-steps to Port 1and Output port 3 is for errors.

 

Regards 

Till

entry.title: how to customize batch quantities on handling units in RF environment
entry.content:
Hello experts

unfortunately I have problems with mix HU in inbound processes. I would like to post goods receipt of batch managed material with different batch numbers all on one HU. 

How do I customize that there could be different batch numbers within one HU.

Thank you in advance!!

entry.title: Re: Document Management service btp trial account
entry.content:
Hi @Subramaniyam,

If take a look at the service description on BTO Discovery Center, you will notice it is not available on trial accounts.

https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/serviceCatalog/document-management-service-application-option?service_plan=standard&region=all&commercialModel=cloud&tab=service_plan

Best regards,
Ivan

entry.title: Re: SQL LTRIM version?
entry.content:
Thanks to all for your posts…

Again, this is not the exact SQL sentence, so my apologies for some unintended typos (ie. “DATA” instead of @DATA. I’m afraid I’m quite aware of the difference).

Sandra:

I posted the code in both formats, as text and as an image. The reason I attached the image was to show that the ABAP editor was displaying that part of the code as an error, even before I activated the code.

Alex (and Sandra!!):

Right!!! I forgot the additional space after and before the parentheses. Nevertheless, as long as the expression is on the same side the error persists so….

Sandra:

You are correct, the expression is valid only on the left side. And that is a shame. In many cases, having the expression on the left side does not allow the DB engine to use a key field, with the corresponding performance issues. Having the expression on the right side would have been, in my particular SQL statement, a lot more efficient.

 

Again, thanks to all for your help. Also, How can I mark Sandra's reply as "Accepted as a solution"?

 

Regards,

Luis

entry.title: Re: PaPM Cloud: how reproduce on-premise run manager func...
entry.content: yes that is correct!

entry.title: Re: Integrate SAP Build Apps and SAP Build Process Automation using SAP BTP Destinations
entry.content: @gabriel_martins - I am also facing same issue with CORS. Could you achieve this POST call to Document information extraction from Build App ?

entry.title: Employee Central - Notification on deleted Global Assignment
entry.content:
Hello,

Could you please help how we could set up a business rule which triggers a notification to EC admin group about a deleted Global Assignment.

Scenario: An employee is sent out abroad for a Global Assignment, a record is created in SuccessFactors EC, but then it needs to be cancelled and the record is deleted from the system (see the screen shot above).

Thank you.

Best regards,

Martina

entry.title: Re: MultiProvider/CompositeProvider scenario in Datasphere
entry.content: Great response as always! Just one correction, a union can have more than 2 views, but the tool makes it seem there is a 2 union limit. To add more than 2 views to a union, drag a new source over the union icon. The right fly-out menu will appear, but don't click anything. Instead, hit the escape key. If all goes well, you should have 3 or more items in the union. We have unioned up to 6 sources this way.

entry.title: Re: Header Material is appearing in PI sheet during Goods...
entry.content:
Hello , I'm currently testing PI sheet solution and I need to add the instruction category for BOM component consumption , I used the standard instruction for consumption and I tried to create also a new one using the instruction message PI_CONS , but after checking the PI sheet , it's always trying to consume the BOM header material and not the BOM items , can you please tell me which instruction configuration did you use to be able to get the components and if there is any configuration behind to do .

Thank you in advance for your help

entry.title: Re: DRF replication of business partners
entry.content:
Hello Michael,

Can a DRFOUT be initiated from a source ECC to a target S/4HANA system. Is this possible? I have a situation where the ECC was converted to S/4HANA and 1-2 BPs were not converted due to DB level inconsistencies. Can DRFOUT solve this problem and even replicate the missing table level entries such as for example address entries missing in ADRV.

Regards,
Aditya

entry.title: Re: PaPM Cloud: how reproduce on-premise run manager func...

entry.title: Re: keep old ABAP versions, ie in Git (?)
entry.content: abapGit route continues here: https://github.com/abapGit/abapGit/issues/6884

entry.title: Re: keep old ABAP versions, ie in Git (?)
entry.content: Ah, I couldn't find any API Docs on https://help.sap.com/docs/ABAP_PLATFORM_NEW/4a368c163b08418890a406d413933ba7/f319b168e87e42149e25e13c08d002b9.html?q=%22api%22%20 , but browsing through classical ABAP did show a nicely structured set of ABAP Packages.

entry.title: Email F150 Dunning letters after reviewing
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

May I know if there is a way to email the dunning letters after reviewing them? 

We have a requirement to view the dunning letters before emailing to exclude some from the mailing list because some customers may have settled their accounts and are not updated. In such cases, their dunning letters shouldn't be emailed.

entry.title: Sending ASN from Freight Order (embedded TM)
entry.content:
Hi all,

we are implementing an embedded SAP-TM on SAP S/4HANA 2022. In this environment we want to send ASNs via Freight Orders to our ship-to-party. 
We checked following blogs, notes:

  • https://community.sap.com/t5/supply-chain-management-blogs-by-sap/sap-advanced-shipping-receiving-advanced-shipping-notification-on/ba-p/13546528
  • https://me.sap.com/notes/0003024670
  • https://community.sap.com/t5/supply-chain-management-q-a/road-freight-order-with-subcontracting-6100002360-skipped/qaq-p/12674451
  • https://community.sap.com/t5/supply-chain-management-q-a/s4hana-and-tm-official-process-to-generate-a-asn-delivery-message-to-your/qaq-p/12592600
  • https://me.sap.com/notes/2469988

As you can see, we investigated really a lot and the xml-message is still not working. Let's have a look into our system.

Freight Order Type has output profile “/SCMTMS/TOR” (Standard):

Inactive flag for Action Definition TOR_TPNOR_ASN is demarked:
And everything depending on this definition is NOT changed. Standard is used.

The output management adapter settings are NOT changed. Standard is used.

Questions:

  • Is there a documentation available how a freight order should look like for sending an ASN (e.g. view "Output Management")?
  • Is there a "how-to" available how to configure the complete solution for sending ASNs from the freight order?
  • Which pre-conditions do we have to consider (e.g. special content of the freight order)?
  • Under which circumstances, status, etc. in the freight order, an ASN should be created?
  • Currently we are using SHPMNT05 in LE-TRA which should be replaced by using the xml-file in TM. If we get a file created, how is the routing to be configured? Today we use WE20 for partner connection sending an IDoc. 

Best Regards,
Thorsten
